Gulf Cartel.description: The Gulf Cartel is a Mexican drug trafficking organization based in Matamoros, Tamaulipas, founded in the 1930s by Juan Nepomuceno Guerra. Initially smuggling alcohol during Prohibition, it later expanded into cocaine, methamphetamine, and marijuana under Juan García Ábrego. It is one of the oldest organized crime groups in Mexico and is designated as a terrorist organization by the United States. The cartel is known for its violence and international network.

Rudaj Organization.description: The Rudaj Organization was an Albanian-American gang active in the New York City metro area, founded in 1993 by Alex Rudaj and Nardino Colotti. It engaged in extortion, illegal gambling, and murder, operating in Queens, The Bronx, and Westchester County. The organization was dismantled after a 2004 federal indictment that charged 22 members.

Los Chapitos.description: Los Chapitos is a splinter faction of the Sinaloa Cartel in Mexico, formed around 2016 by the sons of Joaquín 'El Chapo' Guzmán. The group emerged after Guzmán's capture and extradition, taking control of large parts of the cartel's territory in Sinaloa and neighboring states. They are known for drug trafficking, violence, and conflicts with rival factions and government forces. The group remains active as of the article's last update.
